Notice Of Motion And Pltfs' Motion For Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Carling, defendant appearing in propria persona
Matthew Hindman of Zitrin and Mastromonaco, LLP, 354-2703, appearing for plaintiff

The Court denied Defendant Robert Carling's request for a continuance. The tentative ruling was adopted as follows: PLAINTIFFS STEVEN TAPPE, LINDA SPEGAR, SUSAN MADRID, AND JANET DIEPENBROCK'S Motion For Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement IS GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. Order and Judgment signed in open Court. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189

THE PARTIES PLACE A STIPULATION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE RECORD AS FOLLOWS:

DEFENDANT ROBERT CARLING MUST PAY ADDITIONAL RENT PAYMENTS FOR RENTS DUE AND OWING ON APR-01-06 AND FOR RENT DUE AND OWING ON MAY-01-2006 IN THE AMOUNT OF $200.00 PER MONTH; THE DEFENDANT CURRENTLY OWES $7,578.00 IN PAST DUE RENT. IF THE DEFENDANT ROBERT CARLING VACATES THE PREMISE LOCATED AT 1114 SUTTER STREET, #503, SAN FRANCISCO, CA OR IS EVICTED FROM SAID PREMISE, DEFENDANT CARLING WAIVES THE RIGHT TO ALL PERSONAL PROPERTY LEFT AT THE PREMISES;
DEFENDANT ROBERT CARLING IS TO ALLOW NO OTHER PERSONS OR PERSONS TO RESIDE IN SAID PREMISES WITHOUT PERMISSION OF THE LANDLORD; EACH SIDE IS TO BEAR OWN COSTS; IF DEFENDANT CARLING IS LATE WITH ANY RENT PAYMENTS, PLAINTIFF MAY HAVE ISSUED A THREE-DAY NOTICE TO CURE, AND THE LANDLORD MAY FILE JUDGMENT WITH THE COURT, AS PER SECT. 664.6; THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT WILL RETAIN JURISDICTION OF THE MATTER; ON THE RECORD, DEFENDANT ROBERT CARLING INFORMS THE COURT HE UNDERSTANDS AND IS IN AGREEMENT WITH THE SETTLEMENT TERMS.
